title: Reliability improvement through delay between multi-stage programming steps in non-volatile memory structures
abstract: A method for multi-stage programming of a non-volatile memory structure, wherein the method comprises: (1) initiating a programming operation with respect to a memory block, (2) applying a programming algorithm to the memory block, wherein the programming algorithm comprises at least a first programming stage and a second programming stage, and (3) between the first programming stage and the second programming stage, applying a time delay according to a pre-determined amount of time. Further, the pre-determined amount of time may be defined as the amount of time that, according to a probabilistic function, permits de-trapping of any charges unintentionally trapped within a memory cell of the memory block as a result of the first programming stage.
claims:
Claims CLAIMS What is claimed is: 1. A method for multi-stage programming of a non-volatile memory structure, comprising: initiating a programming operation with respect to a memory block comprising a plurality of NAND-type memory cells; applying a programming algorithm to the memory block, wherein the programming algorithm comprises at least a first programming stage and a second programming stage; and between the first programming stage and the second programming stage, applying a time delay according to a pre-determined amount of time, wherein the pre-determined amount of time is defined as the amount of time that, according to a probabilistic function, permits de-trapping of any charges unintentionally trapped within a memory cell as a result of the first programming stage. 2. The method according to claim 1, wherein: the first programming stage comprises programming at least one programmable state of the memory block according to a first step size; and the second programming stage comprises programming the at least one programmable state of the memory block according to a second step size, wherein a magnitude of the first step size is greater than a magnitude of the second step size. 3. The method according to claim 2, wherein the: first programming stage is applied to a first word line of the memory block according to a programming order; and second programming stage is applied to a word line positioned just prior to the first word line according to the programming order. 4. The method according to claim 1, further comprising applying the time delay after the second programming stage. 5. The method according to claim 1, wherein the time delay is applied only to a specific portion of the memory block. 6. The method according to claim 1, wherein the pre-determined amount of time is further determined according to a desired decrease in a measured error bit rate count of the programming operation. 7. The method according to claim 1, wherein the pre-determined amount of time is in the range of 100 microseconds and five minutes. 8. A memory controller, comprising: a communication pathway configured to couple to a memory block of a non-volatile memory structure, wherein the memory block comprises a plurality of NAND-type memory cells; and the memory controller is configured to: initiate a programming operation with respect to the memory block; apply a programming algorithm to the memory block, wherein the programming algorithm comprises at least a first programming stage and a second programming stage; and between the first programming stage and the second programming stage, apply a time delay according to a pre-determined amount of time, wherein the pre-determined amount of time is defined as the amount of time that, according to a probabilistic function, permits detrapping of any charges unintentionally trapped within a memory cell as a result of the first programming stage. 9. The memory controller according to claim 8, wherein: the first programming stage comprises programming at least one programmable state of the memory block according to a first step size; and 

the second programming stage comprises programming the at least one programmable state of the memory block according to a second step size, wherein a magnitude of the first step size is greater than a magnitude of the second step size. 10. The memory controller according to claim 9, wherein the memory controller is further configured to: apply the first programming stage to a first word line of the memory block according to a programming order; and apply the second programming stage to a word line positioned just prior to the first word line according to the programming order. 11. The memory controller according to claim 8, wherein the memory controller is further configured to apply the time delay after the second programming stage. 12. The memory controller according to claim 8, wherein the pre-determined amount of time is further determined according to a desired decrease in a measured error bit rate count of the programming operation. 13. The memory controller according to claim 8, wherein the time delay is applied only to a specific portion of the memory block. 14. A non-volatile memory system, comprising: a memory block comprising a population of NAND-type memory cells; and a memory controller coupled to the memory array and: initiating a programming operation with respect to the memory block; applying a programming algorithm to the memory block, wherein the programming algorithm comprises at least a first programming stage and a second programming stage; and 

 between the first programming stage and the second programming stage, applying a time delay according to a pre-determined amount of time, wherein the pre-determined amount of time is defined as the amount of time that, according to a probabilistic function, permits de-trapping of any charges unintentionally trapped within a memory cell as a result of the first programming stage. 15. The non-volatile memory system according to claim 14, wherein: the first programming stage comprises programming at least one programmable state of the memory block according to a first step size; and the second programming stage comprises programming the at least one programmable state of the memory block according to a second step size, wherein a magnitude of the first step size is greater than a magnitude of the second step size. 16. The non-volatile memory system according to claim 15, further comprising the memory controller: applying the first programming stage to a first word line of the memory block according to a programming order; and applying the second programming stage to a word line positioned just prior to the first word line according to the programming order. 17. The non-volatile memory system according to claim 14, further comprising applying the time delay after the second programming stage. 18. The non-volatile memory system according to claim 14, wherein the pre-determined amount of time is further determined according to a desired decrease in a measured error bit rate count of the programming operation. 19. The non-volatile memory system according to claim 14, wherein the time delay is applied only to a specific portion of the memory block. 20. The non-volatile memory system according to claim 14, wherein the pre-determined amount of time is in the range of 100 microseconds and five minutes.
